articles.title: Republicans want to add $1 billion for Trump's ballroom security to ICE funding plan
articles.summary: Republicans in Congress are proposing $1 billion in funding for security for President Trump's White House ballroom as part of their partisan plan to fund Immigration and Customs Enforcement.

articles.title: U.S. stages largest-ever annual military drills in the Philippines
articles.summary: U.S. and allies hold their largest drills yet in the Philippines near Taiwan, showcasing new tactics and alliances to deter China—while Beijing stages its own exercises nearby.

articles.title: Attack on French Cargo Ship Highlights Continued Risks in Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: U.S. Central Command and the ship’s owner disagreed on whether the vessel coordinated safe passage with the United States military.

articles.title: Rupert Murdoch, David Zaslav and More Media Moguls Remember Ted Turner, CNN’s Founder
articles.summary: The CNN founder’s death was met with encomiums from leaders in the media industry, which he helped reinvent. “He will be remembered for generations,” said one.

articles.title: Tennessee Republicans Unveil New Congressional Map Carving Up Majority-Black House District
articles.summary: The General Assembly is expected to quickly approve the map, which slices up Memphis, a majority-Black city that makes up most of the state’s lone Democratic district.

articles.title: Ted Turner, Creator of CNN and the 24-Hour News Cycle, Dies at 87
articles.summary: As one of the most important figures in media history, he oversaw a vast cable empire of news, sports and entertainment channels.

articles.title: Higher Gas Prices Are Hitting Lower-Income Americans the Hardest
articles.summary: People with lower incomes are driving less and devoting more of their budgets to transport as gas prices soar.

articles.title: These companies help parents try to pick their babies' traits. Experts are wary
articles.summary: Prospective parents can now pick embryos based on risk predictions for thousands of diseases and odds for specific traits. But should they?

articles.title: Preserving pollinators is good for health -- and income
articles.summary: Pollinators have economic and health benefits, but those benefits have been difficult to quantify. A new study puts some numbers to how important pollinators are for both nutrition and income.

articles.title: How the Fight Over Israel Is Playing Out Inside MAGA
articles.summary: The war in Iran has added to a tectonic shift in public opinion — a bipartisan swing away from Israel. Some on the far-right are fighting to keep President Trump’s movement aligned with the Jewish sta

articles.title: Iran’s Oil Sector and Economy Are Under Pressure as U.S. Blockade Bites
articles.summary: Tehran says it can endure the pain, but its oil may soon have nowhere to go.

articles.title: Why Is Gas So Much More Expensive in Some States Than Others?
articles.summary: Shipping, taxes, competition and environmental regulations all play a part in the cost of gasoline. That can lead to stark differences in pump prices.

articles.title: How Silicon Valley's new tech right has profited by aligning with MAGA
articles.summary: Atlantic writer George Packer discusses how tech venture capitalists, who are heavily invested in AI and cryptocurrency, aligned with Trump and influenced policies related to their own investments.

articles.title: Greetings from a sea village in Indonesia, where Indigenous fishing gets help from mangroves
articles.summary: As a water taxi rounded a corner at sunset, the village of Torosiaje came into view, with its colorful stilted houses perched over the Molucca Sea.

articles.title: 'A trailblazer, a rabble-rouser, a do-gooder': CNN founder Ted Turner dies at 87
articles.summary: Turner had a larger-than-life personality, and used it to launch the country's first 24/7 all-news network.

articles.title: Have you lost or are worried about losing SNAP benefits? NPR wants to hear your story
articles.summary: NPR wants to hear from people impacted by changes to the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program.

articles.title: Iran is reviewing a U.S. proposal as Trump threatens renewed bombing if it doesn't agree
articles.summary: Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesman said negotiators are assessing the Trump administration's latest proposal and will relate a response to mediating country Pakistan.

articles.title: Democrats gain midterm edge, NPR poll shows. And, U.S. pauses 'Project Freedom'
articles.summary: Democrats outpace Republicans in voter enthusiasm for the midterm elections, according to NPR's latest poll. And Trump says "Project Freedom" is paused because of progress toward an Iran agreement.

articles.title: Skeletons in their clothing: Recovering bodies from the rubble in Gaza
articles.summary: It was one of the deadliest Israeli strikes of the Gaza war, devastating one extended family. A year and a half later, the survivors find their loved ones' remains.

articles.title: Zelenskyy slams Russia as strikes kill 22 in Ukraine before announced ceasefire
articles.summary: Zelenskyy rebuked Moscow for what he said was its "utter cynicism" in launching the attacks after Russia announced a unilateral ceasefire over two days later this week.

articles.title: Police say Australian women with alleged IS ties face charges on return from Syria
articles.summary: The Australian government had been alerted Wednesday that four women and nine children had booked flights from Damascus to Australia, Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke said.

articles.title: U.S. says it's made Hormuz safe for commercial shipping, but standoff hasn't changed
articles.summary: The administration has announced a new military strategy designed to open up the Strait of Hormuz to commercial shipping, but it's unclear if this will break Iran's grip on the key waterway.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
